     2	
     3	package main
     4	
     5	import (
     6		"fmt"
     7		"os"
     8		"strconv"
     9		"sync"
    10		"sync/atomic"
    11		"testing"
    12	
    13		"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/testutil"
    14	)
    15	
    16	var (
    17		sharedProxiedOnce  sync.Once
    18		sharedProxiedPort  int
    19		sharedProxiedErr   error
    20		sharedProxiedDBSeq atomic.Int64
    21	)
    22	
    23	func sharedProxiedServerPort(t *testing.T) int {
    24		t.Helper()
    25		sharedProxiedOnce.Do(func() {
    26			if err := testutil.EnsureDoltContainerForTestMain(); err != nil {
    27				sharedProxiedErr = err
    28				return
    29			}
    30			port := testutil.DoltContainerPortInt()
    31			if port == 0 {
    32				sharedProxiedErr = fmt.Errorf("shared dolt container reported no port")
    33				return
    34			}
    35			sharedProxiedPort = port
    36		})
    37		if sharedProxiedErr != nil {
    38			t.Skipf("shared proxied-server unavailable: %v", sharedProxiedErr)
    39		}
    40		return sharedProxiedPort
    41	}
    42	
    43	func requireSharedProxiedServer(t *testing.T) int {
    44		t.Helper()
    45		if os.Getenv("BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_SERVER") != "1" {
    46			t.Skip("set BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_SERVER=1 to run proxied-server integration tests")
    47		}
    48		return sharedProxiedServerPort(t)
    49	}
    50	
    51	func uniqueProxiedDatabase() string {
    52		return fmt.Sprintf("bdtest_%d", sharedProxiedDBSeq.Add(1))
    53	}
    54	
    55	func sharedProxiedInitArgs(t *testing.T, extraInitArgs ...string) (string, []string) {
    56		t.Helper()
    57		port := requireSharedProxiedServer(t)
    58	
    59		database := uniqueProxiedDatabase()
    60		args := append([]string{
    61			"--database", database,
    62			"--proxied-server-external-host", "127.0.0.1",
    63			"--proxied-server-external-port", strconv.Itoa(port),
    64		}, extraInitArgs...)
    65		return database, args
    66	}
    67	
    68	func newSharedProxiedProject(t *testing.T, bd, prefix string, extraInitArgs ...string) proxiedProject {
    69		t.Helper()
    70		database, args := sharedProxiedInitArgs(t, extraInitArgs...)
    71		p := bdProxiedInit(t, bd, prefix, args...)
    72		p.database = database
    73		return p
    74	}
    75	
    76	func newSharedProxiedProjectWithHooks(t *testing.T, bd, prefix string, hooks map[string]string, extraInitArgs ...string) proxiedProject {
    77		t.Helper()
    78		database, args := sharedProxiedInitArgs(t, extraInitArgs...)
    79		p := bdProxiedInitWithHooks(t, bd, prefix, hooks, args...)
    80		p.database = database
    81		return p
    82	}
   100	func checkDolt() doltReadiness {
   101		doltCheckOnce.Do(func() {
   102			// Explicit skip checked first to avoid ~1s docker info cost.
   103			if hasTestSkip("dolt") {
   104				doltCached = doltSkipped
   105				return
   106			}
   107			if !isDockerAvailable() {
   108				return // doltCached zero value is doltNoDocker
   109			}
   110			if isDoltImageCached() {
   111				doltCached = doltReady
   112				return
   113			}
   114			if isDoltRepoImageCached() {
   115				doltCached = doltWrongVersion
   116				return
   117			}
   118			doltCached = doltNoImage
   119		})
   120		return doltCached
   121	}
   122	
   123	// isDoltImageCached returns true if the exact Dolt Docker image (repo:tag)
   124	// is available locally, avoiding unnecessary network calls to Docker Hub.
   125	func isDoltImageCached() bool {
   126		return exec.Command("docker", "image", "inspect", DoltDockerImage).Run() == nil
   127	}
   128	
   129	// isDoltRepoImageCached returns true if ANY version of the Dolt image repo
   130	// exists locally (e.g. dolthub/dolt-sql-server with a different tag).
   131	func isDoltRepoImageCached() bool {
   132		out, err := exec.Command("docker", "images", doltDockerRepo, "-q").Output()
   133		return err == nil && len(strings.TrimSpace(string(out))) > 0
   134	}
   135	
   136	// startDoltContainer starts the singleton Dolt container.
   137	func startDoltContainer() error {
   138		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), serverStartTimeout)
   139		defer cancel()
   140	
   141		ctr, err := dolt.Run(ctx, DoltDockerImage,
   142			dolt.WithDatabase("beads_test"),
   143			// Docker port-forwarding makes connections appear as non-localhost
   144			// (e.g., 172.17.0.1). The entrypoint defaults DOLT_ROOT_HOST to
   145			// "localhost", so root@localhost won't match external connections.
   146			// Set to "%" so root can connect from any host.
   147			testcontainers.WithEnv(map[string]string{"DOLT_ROOT_HOST": "%"}),
   148		)
   149		if err != nil {
   150			return fmt.Errorf("starting Dolt container: %w", err)
   151		}
   152	
   153		p, err := ctr.MappedPort(ctx, "3306/tcp")
   154		if err != nil {
   155			_ = testcontainers.TerminateContainer(ctr)
   156			return fmt.Errorf("getting mapped port: %w", err)
   157		}
   158	
   159		if _, err := strconv.Atoi(p.Port()); err != nil {
   160			_ = testcontainers.TerminateContainer(ctr)
   161			return fmt.Errorf("parsing port %q: %w", p.Port(), err)
   162		}
   163	
   164		doltTestPort = p.Port()
   165		doltSingletonSrv = &doltServer{
   166			container: ctr,
   167		}
   168	
   169		return nil
   170	}
   171	
   172	// terminateSharedContainer stops and removes the shared Dolt container.
   173	// Safe to call concurrently or multiple times (sync.Once).
   174	func terminateSharedContainer() {
   175		doltTerminateOnce.Do(func() {
   176			if doltSingletonSrv != nil && doltSingletonSrv.container != nil {
   177				_ = testcontainers.TerminateContainer(doltSingletonSrv.container)
   178				doltSingletonSrv.container = nil
   179			}
   180		})
   181	}
   182	
   183	// StartIsolatedDoltContainer starts a per-test Dolt container and returns the
   184	// mapped host port. The container is terminated automatically when the test finishes.
   185	func StartIsolatedDoltContainer(t *testing.T) string {
   186		t.Helper()
   187		if state := checkDolt(); state != doltReady {
   188			t.Skipf("skipping test: %s", state)
   189		}
   190	
   191		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), serverStartTimeout)
   192		defer cancel()
   193		ctr, err := dolt.Run(ctx, DoltDockerImage,
   194			dolt.WithDatabase("beads_test"),
   195			testcontainers.WithEnv(map[string]string{"DOLT_ROOT_HOST": "%"}),
   196		)
   197		if err != nil {
   198			t.Fatalf("starting Dolt container: %v", err)
   199		}
   200		t.Cleanup(func() {
   201			if err := testcontainers.TerminateContainer(ctr); err != nil {
   202				t.Logf("terminating Dolt container: %v", err)
   203			}
   204		})
   205	
   206		port, err := ctr.MappedPort(ctx, "3306/tcp")
   207		if err != nil {
   208			t.Fatalf("getting mapped port: %v", err)
   209		}
   210	
   211		portStr := port.Port()
   212		t.Setenv("BEADS_DOLT_PORT", portStr)
   213		return portStr
   214	}
   215	
   216	// ensureSharedContainer starts the singleton container and sets BEADS_DOLT_PORT.
   217	func ensureSharedContainer() {
   218		doltServerOnce.Do(func() {
   219			doltServerErr = startDoltContainer()
   220			if doltServerErr == nil && doltTestPort != "" {
   221				if err := os.Setenv("BEADS_DOLT_PORT", doltTestPort); err != nil {
   222					doltServerErr = fmt.Errorf("set BEADS_DOLT_PORT: %w", err)
   223				}
   224			}
   225		})
   226	}
   227	
   228	// EnsureDoltContainerForTestMain starts a shared Dolt container for use in
   229	// TestMain functions. Call TerminateDoltContainer() after m.Run() to clean up.
   230	// Sets BEADS_DOLT_PORT process-wide.
   231	func EnsureDoltContainerForTestMain() error {
   232		if state := checkDolt(); state != doltReady {
   233			return fmt.Errorf("%s", state)
   234		}
   235	
   236		ensureSharedContainer()
   237		return doltServerErr
   238	}
   239	
   240	// RequireDoltContainer ensures a shared Dolt container is running. Skips the
   241	// test if Docker is not available.
   242	func RequireDoltContainer(t *testing.T) {
   243		t.Helper()
   244		if state := checkDolt(); state != doltReady {
   245			t.Skipf("skipping test: %s", state)
   246		}
   247	
   248		ensureSharedContainer()
   249		if doltServerErr != nil {
   250			t.Fatalf("Dolt container setup failed: %v", doltServerErr)
   251		}
   252	}
   253	
   254	// DoltContainerAddr returns the address (host:port) of the Dolt container.
   255	func DoltContainerAddr() string {
   256		return "127.0.0.1:" + doltTestPort
   257	}
   258

payload.message: A concrete blocker is now reproduced: the new gate does not suppress `cmd/bd`’s package-wide shared-Dolt `TestMain`. On my machine, the “managed-local/no testcontainer” test launched a `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:2.2.0` container before running; the CI log only avoided that because the image was not cached and emitted a warning. In an offline net namespace with that image cached, setup can fail before the target test. The fix is small—teach the test setup that `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL=1` disables the shared container (or equivalently isolate the target binary)—but this is real, not hypothetical.

content.text: A concrete blocker is now reproduced: the new gate does not suppress `cmd/bd`’s package-wide shared-Dolt `TestMain`. On my machine, the “managed-local/no testcontainer” test launched a `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:2.2.0` container before running; the CI log only avoided that because the image was not cached and emitted a warning. In an offline net namespace with that image cached, setup can fail before the target test. The fix is small—teach the test setup that `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL=1` disables the shared container (or equivalently isolate the target binary)—but this is real, not hypothetical.

| Branch | `fix/mybd-o6a-federation` -> `main` |
| State | OPEN, MERGEABLE, no review decision yet |
| Size | +69 / -1 across 3 files |
| Linked issue | gastownhall/beads#3852 (reporter: dlwiii) |
| Local bead | mybd-o6a (P0, v1.0.4 release triage) |
| Reviewer date | 2026-05-27 |

## Verdict

**ready-to-merge** (with one optional follow-up). This is an easy win.

The PR is small, focused, and addresses a real reproducible P0 bug in v1.0.3 / current main. The implementation reuses existing storage-driver primitives (`doltAddAndCommit`, `Commit`, `isDoltNothingToCommit`) and mirrors a well-established pattern already used in `PullFrom` (GH#2474). It lives entirely inside `internal/storage/dolt/`, which is the correct side of the storage-driver boundary — no beads-side code is being asked to know anything new about Dolt. A regression test is included. CI is green (codecov is only flagging that the new lines aren't exercised by non-integration runs, which is expected for tests gated by `//go:build integration`).

## Why it exists

`bd federation add-peer` writes a row into `federation_peers` but does not `DOLT_COMMIT` it. Any subsequent `bd federation sync` triggers `DOLT_FETCH` -> `DOLT_MERGE`, and the merge refuses to proceed with the working set dirty:

```
merge failed: local changes would be stomped by merge: federation_peers
```

The reporter (dlwiii) provided a clean reproducer plus a worrying side-effect note: workarounds involving killing the bg `dolt sql-server` and running `dolt gc` apparently caused silent loss of two locally-created issues. So the fix is on the P0 path because it removes the user's incentive to hand-roll dangerous workarounds.

The current `Sync` also did not perform the pre-merge auto-commit that `PullFrom` (`internal/storage/dolt/federation.go:57-66`) already does. So even if `add-peer` had committed cleanly, any other source of working-set churn between add-peer and sync (e.g. concurrent issue writes) would have hit the same wall.

## What it changes

1. **`internal/storage/dolt/credentials.go:308-311`** — after `AddFederationPeer` upserts the row and registers the Dolt remote, it now calls `s.doltAddAndCommit(ctx, []string{"federation_peers"}, "federation: add peer "+peer.Name)`. The commit is scoped to the single table, matching the GH#2455 pattern used by `dependencies.go:55,88` and `issues.go` callsites.

2. **`internal/storage/dolt/federation.go:294-303`** — `Sync` now does an auto-commit before `Fetch`, with the same shape as `PullFrom`:

   ```go
   if !s.readOnly {
       if err := s.Commit(ctx, "auto-commit before sync"); err != nil {
           if !isDoltNothingToCommit(err) {
               result.Error = fmt.Errorf("failed to commit pending changes before sync: %w", err)
               return result, result.Error
           }
       }
   }
   ```

3. **`internal/storage/dolt/federation_test.go:392-441`** — new `TestFederationSyncCommitsPendingPeerMetadataBeforeFetch`. Adds a peer, asserts `dolt_status` is clean (catches the add-peer bug directly), then dirties `federation_peers` via a raw UPDATE and runs `Sync` against a nonexistent remote; the sync is expected to fail at fetch, but `dolt_status` must be clean afterward (catches the missing pre-merge commit on the Sync side). A small helper `federationStatusHasTable` queries `dolt_status` by table name.

## Strengths

- **Right side of the storage-driver boundary.** All edits land inside `internal/storage/dolt/`. Beads-side packages are untouched. This aligns with the storage-driver roadmap captured in the `beads-storage-driver-roadmap` memory and GH#3617: crash-safety and dirty-working-set handling are the driver's job, and that's where the fix lives.
- **Reuses existing helpers verbatim.** No new commit/staging machinery is invented; the change leans on `doltAddAndCommit` (`store.go:1715-1732`) and `Commit` (`store.go:1630-1690`). `Commit` already correctly excludes `config` to avoid the GH#2455 staging-corruption pattern, so the Sync pre-commit inherits that safety.
- **Mirrors `PullFrom`'s precedent exactly.** Same `readOnly` guard, same `isDoltNothingToCommit` short-circuit, same wrapped error message shape (`federation.go:60-66` vs new `federation.go:294-303`). This makes the change trivially auditable.
- **Honors `readOnly`.** A read-only store will not attempt a commit; behavior degrades gracefully.
- **Targeted commit scope on add-peer.** `doltAddAndCommit(ctx, []string{"federation_peers"}, ...)` only stages the one table, so it cannot sweep up unrelated dirty work into a "federation: add peer X" commit (GH#2455 hygiene).
- **Regression test is asserting the right property.** Probing `dolt_status` directly is the cleanest way to detect a dirty working set without relying on the merge error message.
- **Tight diff.** +69/-1, 3 files, one of them tests. Easy to review, easy to revert.

## Concerns

1. **`internal/storage/dolt/federation.go:294-303` — Sync's pre-commit is broader than add-peer's.** Add-peer commits only `federation_peers`, but Sync's pre-commit calls `s.Commit(ctx, "auto-commit before sync")` which stages every dirty table except `config`. That matches `PullFrom`'s existing behavior, but in the worst case it can produce a commit titled `auto-commit before sync` containing unrelated issue/dependency churn. That's pre-existing behavior in `PullFrom`, not a regression introduced here, and changing it is out of scope for a P0 fix. Worth a follow-up bead to consider scoping the pre-sync commit to federation/metadata tables only, or producing a richer commit message via `CommitPending`-style logic.

2. **`internal/storage/dolt/credentials.go:300-306` — "remote already exists" string-match is unchanged but brittle.** The PR did not touch this, but since AddFederationPeer's commit now follows the AddRemote call, any future variation in the remote-already-exists error string would cause the new commit to be skipped (we'd return before reaching the new code). Pre-existing risk; flag only as a future-hardening note, not a blocker.

3. **`internal/storage/dolt/federation_test.go:1` — test is `//go:build integration`-gated.** That is correct (it touches a live `dolt sql-server`), and matches the rest of the file, but it does mean codecov sees 0% patch coverage on the new lines. The Codecov bot comment is therefore noise, not a real signal. CI passing the integration tag remains the gate; the PR description acknowledges the local skip due to no Docker/Dolt. Not blocking.

4. **`internal/storage/dolt/federation_test.go:430-441` — the helper `federationStatusHasTable` swallows test context via `t.Helper()` but is a free function rather than a method. Fine as-is; consider promoting to a shared test util if more federation tests start querying `dolt_status`.

5. **No CHANGELOG / docs update.** `FEDERATION-SETUP.md` historically had a "if sync fails with stomped federation_peers, run dolt commit" workaround note (per the reporter). If that note still exists, it should be removed or updated. Worth a quick `grep -ri "stomped\|federation_peers" docs/ FEDERATION-SETUP.md` follow-up. Not blocking the merge.

6. **dlwiii's secondary observation about `dolt gc` eating issues is not addressed and shouldn't be.** That's a separate issue (gc safety / docs warning) and rightly out of scope here. Filing a follow-up bead is appropriate.

## Recommendations

- **Merge as-is.** Outcome from PR_MAINTAINER_GUIDELINES.md: this is a clean **Merge** (or arguably **Easy win**). It solves the reported bug, follows the established `PullFrom` precedent, includes a targeted regression test, and is the right shape for the storage-driver-boundary roadmap.
- **File two follow-up beads** (not blocking):
  1. Doc cleanup: scrub `FEDERATION-SETUP.md` for the obsolete `dolt add -A && dolt commit -m flush` workaround, since the fix makes it unnecessary.
  2. `dolt gc` safety: a new bead capturing dlwiii's report that running `dolt gc` against a bd-managed Dolt directory while bd was off appears to have eaten two issues. Whether the resolution is docs, a guard, or a driver-side fix, it shouldn't be lost.
- **On the PR thread,** confirm CI ran the integration tag (so the new test actually exercised the fix), then ship it. If CI did not run integration, the maintainer-side validation should at minimum run `go test -tags=integration ./internal/storage/dolt -run TestFederationSyncCommitsPendingPeerMetadataBeforeFetch -count=1 -v` against a local Dolt server before merge — the PR description notes this was skipped locally.

---

# Codex Cross-Vendor Review of Own Upstream PRs — 2026-07-07

**Bead:** mybd-ycu4 · **Fix follow-up:** mybd-lu6a
**Reviewers:** OpenAI Codex `codex review` (gpt-5.5, high reasoning, read-only) per PR · Claude (fable-5-high) adversarial verification of every finding before posting.
**Scope:** all 15 open maphew-authored PRs on gastownhall/beads in the #4576–#4600 band (#4586 merged before the run and was dropped; #4593 and #4580 joined; #4408/#4206 excluded — tracked by their own beads).

## Why cross-vendor

Same-family review of Claude-assisted PRs has correlated blind spots. AGENTS.md names independent-vendor review as the highest-value Codex use, and this run bore that out: **15 findings, 14 confirmed by independent Claude verification, 1 softened, 0 refuted** — including one P1 data-loss regression that same-family review sweeps had not flagged.

## Mechanics

- One detached git worktree per PR under `.worktrees/beads/codex-rev-<N>`, checked out from local refs `codex-rev/pr-<N>` (the fork's fetch refspec is main-only, so PR branches were fetched into local branch refs, which sibling `fetch --prune` cannot delete).
- Review base pinned as local branch `codex-base` = upstream/main `ce3c040` (post-#4586).
- `scripts/codex-agent reviewer --diff --base codex-base`, concurrency 4 (parallel Codex is sanctioned in beads source worktrees; the coordination repo stays serial).
- Codex tokens bill to the ChatGPT plan and bypass workflow budget accounting; the Claude verification workflow (9 agents) ran under the standing 200k directive.

## Results

| PR | Title (short) | Codex findings | Verification |
|----|---------------|----------------|--------------|
| [#4598](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4598) | bootstrap: retry failed clone cleanup | **P1** cleanup can `RemoveAll` a pre-existing Dolt repo | **confirmed**, end-to-end repro traced |
| [#4592](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4592) | doltserver: reap leaked test servers | P2 Pdeathsig kills subprocess-started servers; P2 SIGKILL without PID revalidation | both **confirmed** (scope corrected: auto-started servers unaffected) |
| [#4576](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4576) | doltremote: canonical comparison hardening | P2 SSH userinfo stripping over-matches; P2 dotless SCP aliases unmatched | confirmed; softened (documented trade-off → P3) |
| [#4595](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4595) | import: respect import.auto=false | P2 `bd config set import.auto false` triggers the import it disables | **confirmed** (env-var workaround exists) |
| [#4600](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4600) | test: port Windows hardening fixes | P2 fixtures moved into source tree; P2 PATH replaced not prepended | both **confirmed** |
| [#4587](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4587) | build: auto gms_pure_go + doctor-build | P2 second `-tags` silently replaces existing; P3 doctor-build substring check | both **confirmed** |
| [#4582](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4582) | docs: advanced page public surface | P2 `bd sql` examples fail in embedded mode | **confirmed** |
| [#4593](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4593) | docs: remove stale worktree guidance | P2 stages nonexistent `.gitattributes`; P2 nonexistent `bd vc conflicts`; P3 broken anchor | all **confirmed** |
| [#4585](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4585) | prime: agent.profile knob | P3 unregistered config key emits false warning | **confirmed** |
| [#4599](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4599) | prune: speed up reference scan | none | clean |
| [#4594](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4594) | ci: golangci-lint pin alignment | none | clean |
| [#4589](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4589) | ci: depguard storage boundary | none | clean |
| [#4588](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4588) | docs: reserved log export | none | clean |
| [#4584](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4584) | test: migration blocker assertions | none | clean |
| [#4580](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4580) | dolt: transaction event parity | none | clean |

## The P1 in brief (#4598)

The new clone-failure cleanup `RemoveAll`s the target whenever `target/.dolt` exists — even when this clone attempt didn't create it. `dolt_database` reaches the clone unvalidated (metadata.json / `BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_DATABASE` verbatim; `ValidateDatabaseName` guards only `bd init` flags and `CREATE DATABASE`), and the pre-clone `doltExists` guard checks only immediate children. A non-basename name like `foo/bar` therefore: clones fine the first time, is missed by the guard the second time, fails `dolt clone` ("target already exists"), and the cleanup deletes the pre-existing repo with its local commits. Pre-PR the same failure was harmless. Hand-edited metadata is exactly the recovery scenario bootstrap serves, so the precondition is not exotic. Fix (mybd-lu6a): validate the name at the top of `BootstrapFromRemoteWithDB` and skip cleanup when the target pre-existed.

## Verification value

Beyond confirm/refute, verification added material the raw findings lacked: the concrete breaking test for #4592 (`TestDoctorCheckHealthReportsVersionMismatchOnRepoLocalPort`) plus a scope correction (auto-started servers are safe); the env-var workaround on #4595; the second unguarded kill window in #4592's sweep; and the re-grading of #4576's dotless-alias finding as a documented trade-off rather than a defect.

## Posted comments

One signed comment per PR with findings (9 total, 2026-07-07): [4598](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4598#issuecomment-4904342221) · [4576](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4576#issuecomment-4904342435) · [4592](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4592#issuecomment-4904342686) · [4595](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4595#issuecomment-4904342881) · [4600](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4600#issuecomment-4904343145) · [4587](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4587#issuecomment-4904343349) · [4582](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4582#issuecomment-4904343577) · [4593](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4593#issuecomment-4904343764) · [4585](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/4585#issuecomment-4904343956). The six clean PRs received no comment (recorded here and in mybd-ycu4 instead).

All comments signed `claude-fable-5-high on behalf of matt wilkie`; Codex authorship of the underlying findings is credited in each comment body.

Message Type: FINAL_ANSWER
Task name: /root
Sender: /root/test_quality_flakes
Payload:
Reviewed exact head `9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f`. No files changed and nothing posted upstream.

Findings:

- P1 — The new CI job can pass while running zero tests. [proxied-local-smoke.yml:66](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:66) never sets `CGO_ENABLED=1`, while both added test files are gated on `cgo` ([helpers:1](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go:1), [lifecycle:1](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:1)). With `CGO_ENABLED=0`, the package still compiles and the exact invocation exits 0 with `PASS` and `testing: warning: no tests to run`. I reproduced this locally. The repository already calls out this silent-neutering class at [.github/workflows/pr.yml:79](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/pr.yml:79). Fix: set `CGO_ENABLED: "1"` on build and run, then assert `-test.list` contains exactly `TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke` before executing it.

- P1 — Path filters omit direct dependencies of the new lane, so relevant regressions will not run it. The workflow filters at [proxied-local-smoke.yml:19](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:19) and [line 29](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:29), but the test directly reuses `bdProxiedRun`, `bdProxiedInit`, `bdProxiedCreate`, and `bdProxiedShow` from [proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:53](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:53) and [line 294](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:294); that file does not match any filter. Neither do `cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go`, `internal/configfile/**`, `go.mod`, or several general init/config paths. A change that makes the shared helper select an external server, drops required environment isolation, or changes client-info decoding can therefore merge without exercising this lane. Fix: run this small job on every PR/push, or maintain a complete dependency set and include `merge_group`.

- P2 — The “no testcontainer/offline” lane still initializes the package’s Docker testcontainer hook. The offline step at [proxied-local-smoke.yml:71](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:71) does not set `BEADS_TEST_SKIP=dolt`, while [test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:15](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:15) registers a hook that only exempts embedded and external-proxy modes, not `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL` ([line 29](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:29)). My exact local smoke run started and stopped `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:2.2.0`; the hosted CI log happened to say the image was not cached and skipped it. Because Docker is reached over a Unix socket, the daemon is outside the test’s network namespace and can create networked containers despite the `unshare --net` probe. Fix both layers: exempt `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL` in the package hook and set `BEADS_TEST_SKIP=dolt` in this workflow.

- P2 — Dynamic topology checks do not prove the advertised serving ports belong to the advertised processes. [assertLoopbackOnlyListeners:175](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:175) requires only “at least one listener” and that every discovered listener is loopback. Calls at [lines 100–101](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:100) never pass `proxyPF.Port` or `backendPF.Port`. A PID with an unrelated loopback listener satisfies the dynamic assertion while the advertised port is owned elsewhere—the exact kind of gross PID/socket mismatch this inspection intends to catch. Fix: require the process inode set to contain `127.0.0.1:<expected pidfile port>` in addition to rejecting non-loopback listeners.

- P2 — Poll deadlines do not bound the subprocesses they invoke. `openHeldManagedProxiedConn` advertises a 60-second wait at [helpers:209](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go:209), but its condition calls `bdProxiedRun` at [line 214](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go:214), which uses unbounded `exec.Command(...).Run()` at [proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:53](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:53). Init/create/show have the same exposure. A wedged CLI bypasses all local deadlines and reaches the 15-minute Go timeout; Go timeout cleanup is not a safe guardian for detached proxy/backend children. Fix: use context-bounded subprocess helpers for this managed lane, with diagnostic output and explicit process-tree cleanup.

- P3 — The restarted-PID inequality is a legitimate-flake assertion. [lifecycle:141](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:141) fails if Linux legitimately reuses the old PID. The test already proves the old process died and the new pidfile names a live serving process; numerical inequality is not an identity contract. Drop it, or compare process birth tokens if identity coherence is intentionally brought into scope.

- P3 — Failure diagnostics are discarded. The interesting `server.log`, generated config, and pidfiles live under `t.TempDir`, while the workflow has no `if: failure()` artifact upload. An intermittent launch/idle-shutdown failure will leave only the final poll-state message. Preserve the proxy root and process/socket snapshots as a failure artifact.

On size/reuse: the 22-second end-to-end scenario is valuable, but all 16 newly added helper/parser symbols are currently used only by this one smoke test. The roughly 100-line procfs parser at [lifecycle:193](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:193) has no table tests for IPv4, IPv6, mapped addresses, malformed rows, or missing `tcp6`. The 627 lines are therefore not yet justified by demonstrated reuse. I would keep the lifecycle coverage, add exact-port/parser tests, and either extract the procfs code into a genuinely shared test utility or stop presenting speculative future reuse as present value.

Strengths:

- The dedicated environment gate fails, rather than skips, when local Dolt is missing.
- Managed-local initialization explicitly rejects an `External` block.
- Holding and then closing the entire SQL pool correctly prevents pooled TCP sessions from masking idle shutdown.
- Idle shutdown checks both pidfile removal and actual process death.
- Static generated-config validation, live socket inspection, SQL through the proxy, restart, and persisted reread are complementary rather than redundant.
- Cleanup is registered before init launches the detached topology.
- The exact smoke passed locally in 23.3 seconds and passed hosted CI in 21.8 seconds.
- I tried to falsify the held-connection lifecycle, IPv4 procfs decoding, idle timing, and ordinary cleanup; those behaved soundly.

Message Type: FINAL_ANSWER
Task name: /root
Sender: /root/lifecycle_semantics
Payload:
- **P1 — A stale `proxy.pid` can route commands to an unrelated local service.** `internal/storage/dbproxy/proxy/endpoint.go:117-125` accepts `readAndDial`, while `endpoint.go:305-323` only reads the port and checks that *something* accepts TCP. It never validates `Pid`, the proxy lock, process start identity, or protocol. `internal/storage/dbproxy/proxy/endpoint_mismatch_test.go:72-91` even codifies reuse with bogus PID `12345` and an arbitrary listener. After SIGKILL plus port reuse, managed mode can connect to another MySQL/Dolt instance and run schema initialization against the wrong server. Require a held proxy lock plus PID/start-token or nonce handshake before reuse; reject legacy/unverifiable pidfiles and add stale-pidfile/occupied-port tests.

- **P1 — Backend readiness can be satisfied by a listener that is not the Dolt child just launched.** `cmd/bd/proxied_server.go:352-370` selects and persists a free port without reserving it. `internal/storage/dbproxy/server/doltserver.go:264-281` starts the child and reaps it asynchronously, but `doltserver.go:293-305` declares readiness after a bare TCP dial. If the port is occupied at restart, the new Dolt process may still be alive briefly while failing its bind; `waitReady` connects to the occupant and returns success before `cmd.Wait` cancels `egCtx`. A compatible MySQL/Dolt occupant can then receive Beads migrations/data. Recheck child liveness after a stabilization interval and perform a MySQL/Dolt identity probe tied to the expected root; add a test that occupies the configured backend port before `Start`.

- **P1 — Concurrent restart and `bd dolt stop` can kill an unrelated reused PID.** When the backend lock is free, `internal/storage/dbproxy/proxy/endpoint.go:162-168` releases it without deleting a stale `proxy-child.pid`. A new backend then holds that lock while running configure/init, before overwriting the pidfile at `internal/storage/dbproxy/server/doltserver.go:221-268`. During that window, `internal/storage/dbproxy/proxy/shutdown.go:61-72` sees the held lock and blindly SIGKILLs the stale recorded PID. After PID reuse, that is an unrelated same-user process. Remove the stale backend pidfile while the lock is proven free, and add a process start token/nonce that `Shutdown` must validate before signaling. Add a stale-pidfile + held-startup-lock race test.

- **P2 — Idle accounting can miss recent activity or race a just-accepted connection.** `internal/storage/dbproxy/proxy/server.go:202-231` samples `activeConns` only on ticks, so a complete connection between ticks never resets `idleSince`. Also, `server.go:258-270` accepts a socket and schedules a goroutine before incrementing the counter, allowing the expiry tick to cancel a newly accepted client. Track last activity through accept/close events rather than polling snapshots, and increment before handing the connection to a goroutine. Add synchronized tests for a short connection just before expiry and an accept concurrent with expiry.

- **P2 — The smoke test can pass with broken idle-timeout propagation and active-connection gating.** The helper loads client info but checks only `External` at `cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go:62-71`; it never asserts the requested timeout. The test requests 5s at `cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:48-56`, never deliberately holds the connection beyond a full timeout, then allows 90s for shutdown at `lifecycle_linux_test.go:121-124`. Dropping the flag propagation to the 30s default still passes, and broken active gating passes whenever inspection finishes before expiry. Assert `info.IdleTimeout == 5s`, hold/query across at least one full timeout, and separately assert the proxy’s idle-expiry event occurs within a tight window while retaining a generous backend-GC cleanup allowance.

- **P2 — Shutdown/restart assertions do not exclude an untracked surviving backend.** `cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go:158-173` declares shutdown from pidfile absence and death of the two recorded PIDs only; it does not prove the old proxy/backend ports stopped listening. After restart, `cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:134-147` inspects only the new proxy, not a new backend pidfile/process. An orphaned/forked backend could survive under another PID and, combined with the TCP-only readiness bug, serve the restart while the smoke stays green. Before restart, require both recorded ports to refuse connections; afterward require a fresh backend identity and listener owned by that backend.

- **P2 — Relevant production changes can bypass the new workflow entirely.** `.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:19-37` omits `cmd/bd/init.go`, although idle flag parsing, validation, and dispatch live at `cmd/bd/init.go:298-380`, `init.go:500`, and `init.go:2055`. It also omits `internal/configfile/proxied_server_client_info.go` and `internal/doltserver/**`, both on this lifecycle path. A PR breaking flag persistence or sidecar/root resolution would not trigger the lane. Add those paths or broaden the filter to all managed-proxy lifecycle dependencies.

- **P3 — The supposedly testcontainer-free lane can still start TestMain’s shared container.** The workflow sets only the local lane variable at `.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:73-88`, but `cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:29-39` bypasses container setup only for embedded or external-proxied variables, not `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL`. With a cached image, TestMain attempts a Docker container from inside the offline netns, adding side effects and up to a 60s wait. Bypass the hook for the local lane or pass `BEADS_TEST_SKIP=dolt`.

- **P3 — Numeric PID inequality is a flaky restart identity assertion.** `cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go:99-110` treats any live holder of a number as the old process, while `cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:141-143` rejects legitimate PID reuse. Capture `/proc/<pid>/stat` start time/executable/root identity before shutdown and compare identities, not PID numbers.

What held up under falsification:

- The test genuinely routes CLI writes through managed proxied mode: subprocess environment selection, `External == nil`, and the direct SQL reread through the advertised proxy jointly prevent an embedded-store false positive.
- `heldProxiedConn.Release` correctly closes both the checked-out connection and pool, with a cleanup backstop.
- Listener enumeration correctly joins process socket inodes to both `/proc/net/tcp` and `/proc/net/tcp6`; the address decoding and loopback checks are sound.
- The normal graceful proxy path closes the listener, drains connection handlers, stops the backend, and removes pidfiles in the right order.
- Targeted proxy idle/endpoint tests passed, and the new cmd/bd test sources compile at exact head `9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f`; the worktree remained clean.

Findings:

- P1 — The new CI job can pass while running zero tests. [proxied-local-smoke.yml:66](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:66) never sets `CGO_ENABLED=1`, while both added test files are gated on `cgo` ([helpers:1](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go:1), [lifecycle:1](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:1)). With `CGO_ENABLED=0`, the package still compiles and the exact invocation exits 0 with `PASS` and `testing: warning: no tests to run`. I reproduced this locally. - P1 — Path filters omit direct dependencies of the new lane, so relevant regressions will not run it. The workflow filters at [proxied-local-smoke.yml:19](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:19) and [line 29](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:29), but the test directly reuses `bdProxiedRun`, `bdProxiedInit`, `bdProxiedCreate`, and `bdProxiedShow` from [proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:53](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:53) and [line 294](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:294); that file does not match any filter. Neither do `cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go`, `internal/configfile/**`, `go.mod`, or several general init/config paths. A change that makes the shared helper select an external server, drops required environment isolation, or changes client-info decoding can therefore merge without exercising this lane. Fix: run this small job on every PR/push, or maintain a complete dependency set and include `merge_group`.

- P2 — The “no testcontainer/offline” lane still initializes the package’s Docker testcontainer hook. The offline step at [proxied-local-smoke.yml:71](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:71) does not set `BEADS_TEST_SKIP=dolt`, while [test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:15](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:15) registers a hook that only exempts embedded and external-proxy modes, not `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL` ([line 29](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:29)). Fix both layers: exempt `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL` in the package hook and set `BEADS_TEST_SKIP=dolt` in this workflow.

Calls at [lines 100–101](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:100) never pass `proxyPF.Port` or `backendPF.Port`. Init/create/show have the same exposure. A wedged CLI bypasses all local deadlines and reaches the 15-minute Go timeout; Go timeout cleanup is not a safe guardian for detached proxy/backend children. Fix: use context-bounded subprocess helpers for this managed lane, with diagnostic output and explicit process-tree cleanup.

- P3 — The restarted-PID inequality is a legitimate-flake assertion. [lifecycle:141](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:141) fails if Linux legitimately reuses the old PID. The test already proves the old process died and the new pidfile names a live serving process; numerical inequality is not an identity contract. Drop it, or compare process birth tokens if identity coherence is intentionally brought into scope.

An intermittent launch/idle-shutdown failure will leave only the final poll-state message. Preserve the proxy root and process/socket snapshots as a failure artifact.

On size/reuse: the 22-second end-to-end scenario is valuable, but all 16 newly added helper/parser symbols are currently used only by this one smoke test. The roughly 100-line procfs parser at [lifecycle:193](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:193) has no table tests for IPv4, IPv6, mapped addresses, malformed rows, or missing `tcp6`. The 627 lines are therefore not yet justified by demonstrated reuse. I would keep the lifecycle coverage, add exact-port/parser tests, and either extract the procfs code into a genuinely shared test utility or stop presenting speculative future reuse as present value.

Strengths:

- The dedicated environment gate fails, rather than skips, when local Dolt is missing.
- Managed-local initialization explicitly rejects an `External` block.
- Holding and then closing the entire SQL pool correctly prevents pooled TCP sessions from masking idle shutdown.
- Idle shutdown checks both pidfile removal and actual process death.
- Static generated-config validation, live socket inspection, SQL through the proxy, restart, and persisted reread are complementary rather than redundant.
- Cleanup is registered before init launches the detached topology.
- The exact smoke passed locally in 23.3 seconds and passed hosted CI in 21.8 seconds.
- I tried to falsify the held-connection lifecycle, IPv4 procfs decoding, idle timing, and ordinary cleanup; those behaved soundly."}}]}

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/mybd/pr-5004-adversarial/reports/pr-5004-adversarial-review-2026-07-23.md
+# PR #5004 Adversarial Review — managed-local proxied lifecycle smoke
+
+| Field | Value |
+|---|---|
+| PR | [gastownhall/beads#5004](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/5004) |
+| Title | `test: managed-local proxied lifecycle smoke lane (Linux, offline)` |
+| Author | maphew |
+| Base / head | `main@1125ef3b` / `test/proxied-local-smoke@9bcfcbff` |
+| Size | +627 / -0 across 3 files |
+| Local bead | `mybd-87ef` |
+| Review date | 2026-07-23 |
+| Review method | Three independent `gpt-5.6-sol` / xhigh read-only reviews plus orchestrator reproduction and code tracing |
+
+## Verdict
+
+**Fix-merge. Do not merge the current head as-is.**
+
+The lane is valuable and the central test design is sound: it genuinely selects
+managed-local mode, launches the local proxy/backend topology, writes and reads
+through it, checks generated configuration and live listeners, observes idle
+shutdown, and verifies transparent restart with persisted data. The exact smoke
+passed three consecutive local runs against Dolt 2.2.0 (66.1 seconds total), and
+the hosted lane passed against pinned Dolt 2.2.2.
+
+The current head nevertheless has three CI-integrity blockers. The new job can
+silently pass while running no target test, skips direct dependencies through
+its path filters, and still invokes the package-wide Docker testcontainer setup
+despite claiming an offline/testcontainer-free lane. These are small fixes on a
+useful contributor branch, so the maintainer-policy outcome is **Fix-merge**,
+not rejection or replacement.
+
+## Merge blockers
+
+### P1 — CGO-off builds silently pass with zero managed-local tests
+
+Both added Go files require `cgo`, but the workflow neither sets
+`CGO_ENABLED=1` nor proves the compiled binary contains
+`TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke`:
+
+- [workflow build/run](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml#L66-L89)
+- [helper build constraint](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go#L1)
+- [lifecycle build constraint](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L1)
+
+Reproduction at the exact PR head:
+
+```text
+$ CGO_ENABLED=0 go test -tags gms_pure_go -count=1 \
+    -run '^TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke$' -v ./cmd/bd
+testing: warning: no tests to run
+PASS
+ok github.com/steveyegge/beads/cmd/bd 0.075s [no tests to run]
+```
+
+GitHub's current Ubuntu image defaults CGO on, so the observed PR run did
+execute the test. The workflow is still fail-open: a runner/toolchain/env change
+turns the dedicated gate into a green no-op.
+
+**Fix:** set `CGO_ENABLED: "1"` explicitly for compilation and execution, then
+fail unless `-test.list '^TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke$'` returns the
+expected test before running it.
+
+### P1 — Path filters omit direct managed-local dependencies
+
+The workflow filters cover the new files, selected proxied command files,
+`dbproxy`, and `uow`, but omit direct lifecycle inputs including:
+
+- `cmd/bd/init.go` — parses, validates, and dispatches
+  `--proxied-server-idle-timeout`;
+- `cmd/bd/init_proxied_server.go` — builds and persists the proxied client info;
+- `cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go` — provides every subprocess
+  init/create/show helper used by the new test;
+- `cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go` — controls package-wide pre-test setup;
+- `cmd/bd/store_factory*.go`, `internal/configfile/**`, and
+  `internal/doltserver/**` — select and configure the managed path;
+- `go.mod` / `go.sum` — can change the embedded/client and pinned external-Dolt
+  compatibility surface.
+
+See the current [push and pull-request filters](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml#L17-L37).
+A change that drops idle-timeout persistence, changes client-info decoding, or
+misroutes managed init can therefore merge without exercising the only
+managed-local lane.

+The workflow also has no `merge_group` trigger, so it cannot become a reliable
+merge-queue gate in its present form.

+after its binary is built. If cost requires filtering, include the complete
+direct dependency set above and add `merge_group`.

+`BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_SERVER=1`, but not for the new
+`BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL=1` mode:

+
+- the exact local smoke invocation started and stopped
+  `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:2.2.0` before the managed-local test;
+- the hosted CI log avoided startup only because that image was not cached:
+  `WARN: Docker image ... not cached locally ..., skipping Dolt tests`.
+
+Docker is reached through its Unix socket, so the daemon is outside the
+test process's network namespace. A cached image makes this allegedly
+testcontainer-free lane environment-dependent and allows unrelated Docker
+setup to delay or fail before the target test.
+
+**Fix:** exempt `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL=1` in
+`startTestDoltServer`, and set `BEADS_TEST_SKIP=dolt` in the workflow as a
+defense-in-depth assertion of intent.
+
+## Important test-strengthening fixes
+
+### P2 — Dynamic listener checks do not bind the pidfile ports to the pidfile processes

+`assertLoopbackOnlyListeners` requires at least one listener and rejects
+non-loopback addresses, but it never receives the expected `proxyPF.Port` or
+`backendPF.Port`. A PID with an unrelated loopback listener passes even when the
+advertised serving port belongs to another process.

+See [the calls](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L94-L102)
+and [the assertion](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L175-L190).

+**Fix:** require the enumerated socket set to contain the expected
+`127.0.0.1:<pidfile port>` for each process, in addition to rejecting every
+non-loopback listener.

+

+client-info `External` block is absent. It never asserts
+`info.IdleTimeout == 5*time.Second`. The shutdown wait permits 90 seconds, so a
+regression that drops the flag and falls back to the 30-second production
+default still passes.

+and [timeout setup/wait](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L44-L56).

+connection is intended to prove active-connection gating end to end, hold and
+query it across more than one full configured idle interval before release.

+
+- Use context-bounded subprocess helpers. The outer 60-second poll deadline
+  does not bound `bdProxiedRun`, which uses an unbounded `exec.Command.Run`;
+  a wedged child reaches the 15-minute global timeout and can strand detached
+  proxy/backend processes.
+- Remove the `proxyPF2.Pid != proxyPF.Pid` assertion or compare process birth
+  identities. Linux may legitimately reuse a numeric PID; number inequality is
+  not an identity contract.
+- Add table tests for the roughly 100-line procfs parser, especially IPv6,
+  mapped addresses, malformed rows, and absent `/proc/net/tcp6`.
+- Upload `server.log`, generated config, pidfiles, and socket snapshots on
+  workflow failure. The current `t.TempDir` cleanup discards the most useful
+  diagnostics for an intermittent lifecycle failure.

+

+port-readiness, and shutdown races in the managed proxy implementation. PR
+#5004 does not introduce them, and its body explicitly leaves identity

+pidfile-port ownership and the restarted backend identity. Those assertions
+strengthen this lane without pretending to solve the underlying identity
+protocol.
+

+
+- `External == nil`, proxied subprocess environment selection, and direct SQL
+  through the advertised proxy jointly rule out an embedded/external false
+  positive.
+- Closing both the checked-out `sql.Conn` and its entire `sql.DB` pool is the
+  correct way to release every proxy TCP session before idle shutdown.
+- Cleanup is registered before init launches the detached topology.
+- The graceful path checks both pidfile removal and death of the recorded proxy
+  and backend processes.
+- Static generated-config validation, live socket enumeration, CLI CRUD,
+  direct SQL, idle shutdown, restart, and persisted reread provide complementary
+  signal.
+- The `/proc/net/tcp{,6}` inode join and address decoding are sound for the
+  current Linux/amd64 lane.
+- `git diff --check` is clean; formatting and lint CI passed.
+
+## Validation and PR state observed
+
+- Base `main` health: green under blocking preflight.
+- Preflight: branch maintainable and mergeable, but review required and checks
+  pending at review start.
+- Hosted managed-local lane: passed in 21.8 seconds on Dolt 2.2.2.
+- Local exact smoke: three consecutive passes on Dolt 2.2.0, 66.1 seconds
+  total; package setup unexpectedly started the shared Docker Dolt container.
+- CGO-off falsification: green zero-test reproduction confirmed.
+- PR discussion/review threads: none at review time.
+- Working tree at exact head `9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f`
+  remained clean.
+
+## Recommended maintainer action
+
+Apply the three blocker fixes directly on `test/proxied-local-smoke`, strengthen
+the exact-port and timeout assertions in the same pass, rerun the hosted lane,
+and obtain a substantive review from someone other than the author. The PR is
+then a strong merge candidate.
+
+Do not open a replacement PR: the contributor branch is writable, the design
+and most of the implementation are worth preserving, and every blocker is
+localized.

# PR #5004 Adversarial Review — managed-local proxied lifecycle smoke

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| PR | [gastownhall/beads#5004](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/5004) |
| Title | `test: managed-local proxied lifecycle smoke lane (Linux, offline)` |
| Author | maphew |

| Size | +627 / -0 across 3 files |
| Local bead | `mybd-87ef` |

| Review method | Three independent `gpt-5.6-sol` / xhigh read-only reviews plus orchestrator reproduction and code tracing |

## Verdict

**Fix-merge. Do not merge the current head as-is.**

The lane is valuable and the central test design is sound: it genuinely selects
managed-local mode, launches the local proxy/backend topology, writes and reads
through it, checks generated configuration and live listeners, observes idle
shutdown, and verifies transparent restart with persisted data. The exact smoke
passed three consecutive local runs against Dolt 2.2.0 (66.1 seconds total), and
the hosted lane passed against pinned Dolt 2.2.2.

The current head nevertheless has three CI-integrity blockers. The new job can
silently pass while running no target test, skips direct dependencies through
its path filters, and still invokes the package-wide Docker testcontainer setup
despite claiming an offline/testcontainer-free lane. These are small fixes on a
useful contributor branch, so the maintainer-policy outcome is **Fix-merge**,
not rejection or replacement.

## Merge blockers

### P1 — CGO-off builds silently pass with zero managed-local tests

Both added Go files require `cgo`, but the workflow neither sets
`CGO_ENABLED=1` nor proves the compiled binary contains
`TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke`:

- [workflow build/run](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml#L66-L89)
- [helper build constraint](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go#L1)
- [lifecycle build constraint](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L1)

Reproduction at the exact PR head:

```text
$ CGO_ENABLED=0 go test -tags gms_pure_go -count=1 \
    -run '^TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke$' -v ./cmd/bd
testing: warning: no tests to run
PASS
ok github.com/steveyegge/beads/cmd/bd 0.075s [no tests to run]
```

GitHub's current Ubuntu image defaults CGO on, so the observed PR run did
execute the test. The workflow is still fail-open: a runner/toolchain/env change
turns the dedicated gate into a green no-op.

**Fix:** set `CGO_ENABLED: "1"` explicitly for compilation and execution, then
fail unless `-test.list '^TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke$'` returns the
expected test before running it.

### P1 — Path filters omit direct managed-local dependencies

The workflow filters cover the new files, selected proxied command files,
`dbproxy`, and `uow`, but omit direct lifecycle inputs including:

- `cmd/bd/init.go` — parses, validates, and dispatches
  `--proxied-server-idle-timeout`;
- `cmd/bd/init_proxied_server.go` — builds and persists the proxied client info;
- `cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go` — provides every subprocess
  init/create/show helper used by the new test;
- `cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go` — controls package-wide pre-test setup;
- `cmd/bd/store_factory*.go`, `internal/configfile/**`, and
  `internal/doltserver/**` — select and configure the managed path;
- `go.mod` / `go.sum` — can change the embedded/client and pinned external-Dolt
  compatibility surface.

See the current [push and pull-request filters](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml#L17-L37).

misroutes managed init can therefore merge without exercising the only
managed-local lane.

merge-queue gate in its present form.

direct dependency set above and add `merge_group`.

`BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL=1` mode:

- [package hook registration and bypasses](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/1125ef3b46b8436bb5132318f56f62c593fcc403/cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go#L15-L39)

This is observable, not theoretical:

- the exact local smoke invocation started and stopped
  `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:2.2.0` before the managed-local test;
- the hosted CI log avoided startup only because that image was not cached:
  `WARN: Docker image ... not cached locally ..., skipping Dolt tests`.

Docker is reached through its Unix socket, so the daemon is outside the
test process's network namespace. A cached image makes this allegedly
testcontainer-free lane environment-dependent and allows unrelated Docker
setup to delay or fail before the target test.

regression that drops the flag and falls back to the 30-second production

See [client-info validation](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go#L52-L72)
and [timeout setup/wait](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L44-L56).

## Non-blocking hardening

- Use context-bounded subprocess helpers. The outer 60-second poll deadline

With `CGO_ENABLED=0`, the package still compiles and the exact invocation exits 0 with `PASS` and `testing: warning: no tests to run`. I reproduced this locally. The workflow filters at [proxied-local-smoke.yml:19](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:19) and [line 29](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:29), but the test directly reuses `bdProxiedRun`, `bdProxiedInit`, `bdProxiedCreate`, and `bdProxiedShow` from [proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:53](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:53) and [line 294](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go:294); that file does not match any filter. Neither do `cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go`, `internal/configfile/**`, `go.mod`, or several general init/config paths. A change that makes the shared helper select an external server, drops required environment isolation, or changes client-info decoding can therefore merge without exercising this lane. Fix: run this small job on every PR/push, or maintain a complete dependency set and include `merge_group`.

- P2 — The “no testcontainer/offline” lane still initializes the package’s Docker testcontainer hook. The offline step at [proxied-local-smoke.yml:71](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml:71) does not set `BEADS_TEST_SKIP=dolt`, while [test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:15](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:15) registers a hook that only exempts embedded and external-proxy modes, not `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL` ([line 29](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go:29)). Because Docker is reached over a Unix socket, the daemon is outside the test’s network namespace and can create networked containers despite the `unshare --net` probe. The roughly 100-line procfs parser at [lifecycle:193](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/beads/pr-5004-review/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go:193) has no table tests for IPv4, IPv6, mapped addresses, malformed rows, or missing `tcp6`. The 627 lines are therefore not yet justified by demonstrated reuse. I would keep the lifecycle coverage, add exact-port/parser tests, and either extract the procfs code into a genuinely shared test utility or stop presenting speculative future reuse as present value.

Strengths:

- The dedicated environment gate fails, rather than skips, when local Dolt is missing.
- Managed-local initialization explicitly rejects an `External` block.
- Holding and then closing the entire SQL pool correctly prevents pooled TCP sessions from masking idle shutdown.
- Idle shutdown checks both pidfile removal and actual process death.

- Cleanup is registered before init launches the detached topology.
- The exact smoke passed locally in 23.3 seconds and passed hosted CI in 21.8 seconds.
- I tried to falsify the held-connection lifecycle, IPv4 procfs decoding, idle timing, and ordinary cleanup; those behaved soundly."}}]}

During that window, `internal/storage/dbproxy/proxy/shutdown.go:61-72` sees the held lock and blindly SIGKILLs the stale recorded PID. After PID reuse, that is an unrelated same-user process. ## review/pr-5004-adversarial
?? reports/pr-5004-adversarial-review-2026-07-23.md
# PR #5004 Adversarial Review — managed-local proxied lifecycle smoke

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| PR | [gastownhall/beads#5004](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/pull/5004) |
| Title | `test: managed-local proxied lifecycle smoke lane (Linux, offline)` |
| Author | maphew |
| Base / head | `main@1125ef3b` / `test/proxied-local-smoke@9bcfcbff` |
| Size | +627 / -0 across 3 files |
| Local bead | `mybd-87ef` |
| Review date | 2026-07-23 |
| Review method | Three independent review angles fanned out; two `gpt-5.6-sol` / xhigh reviews completed, one slow run was stopped; orchestrator reproduced and reconciled findings |

## Verdict

**Fix-merge. Do not merge the current head as-is.**

The lane is valuable and the central test design is sound: it genuinely selects
managed-local mode, launches the local proxy/backend topology, writes and reads
through it, checks generated configuration and live listeners, observes idle
shutdown, and verifies transparent restart with persisted data. The exact smoke
passed three consecutive local runs against Dolt 2.2.0 (66.1 seconds total), and
the hosted lane passed against pinned Dolt 2.2.2.

The current head nevertheless has three CI-integrity blockers. The new job can
silently pass while running no target test, skips direct dependencies through
its path filters, and still invokes the package-wide Docker testcontainer setup
despite claiming an offline/testcontainer-free lane. These are small fixes on a
useful contributor branch, so the maintainer-policy outcome is **Fix-merge**,
not rejection or replacement.

## Merge blockers

### P1 — CGO-off builds silently pass with zero managed-local tests

Both added Go files require `cgo`, but the workflow neither sets
`CGO_ENABLED=1` nor proves the compiled binary contains
`TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke`:

- [workflow build/run](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml#L66-L89)
- [helper build constraint](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go#L1)
- [lifecycle build constraint](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L1)

Reproduction at the exact PR head:

```text
$ CGO_ENABLED=0 go test -tags gms_pure_go -count=1 \
    -run '^TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke$' -v ./cmd/bd
testing: warning: no tests to run
PASS
ok github.com/steveyegge/beads/cmd/bd 0.075s [no tests to run]
```

GitHub's current Ubuntu image defaults CGO on, so the observed PR run did
execute the test. The workflow is still fail-open: a runner/toolchain/env change
turns the dedicated gate into a green no-op.

**Fix:** set `CGO_ENABLED: "1"` explicitly for compilation and execution, then
fail unless `-test.list '^TestManagedLocalProxiedLifecycleSmoke$'` returns the
expected test before running it.

### P1 — Path filters omit direct managed-local dependencies

The workflow filters cover the new files, selected proxied command files,
`dbproxy`, and `uow`, but omit direct lifecycle inputs including:

- `cmd/bd/init.go` — parses, validates, and dispatches
  `--proxied-server-idle-timeout`;
- `cmd/bd/init_proxied_server.go` — builds and persists the proxied client info;
- `cmd/bd/proxied_integration_helpers_test.go` — provides every subprocess
  init/create/show helper used by the new test;
- `cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go` — controls package-wide pre-test setup;
- `cmd/bd/store_factory*.go`, `internal/configfile/**`, and
  `internal/doltserver/**` — select and configure the managed path;
- `go.mod` / `go.sum` — can change the embedded/client and pinned external-Dolt
  compatibility surface.

See the current [push and pull-request filters](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml#L17-L37).
A change that drops idle-timeout persistence, changes client-info decoding, or
misroutes managed init can therefore merge without exercising the only
managed-local lane.

The workflow also has no `merge_group` trigger, so it cannot become a reliable
merge-queue gate in its present form.

**Fix:** preferably run this roughly 20-second runtime test on every PR/push
after its binary is built. If cost requires filtering, include the complete
direct dependency set above and add `merge_group`.

### P1 — The “no testcontainer” lane still enters shared Docker setup

`cmd/bd` registers a CGO package hook that starts the shared Dolt testcontainer.
It bypasses that setup for `BEADS_TEST_EMBEDDED_DOLT=1` and
`BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_SERVER=1`, but not for the new
`BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL=1` mode:

- [package hook registration and bypasses](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/1125ef3b46b8436bb5132318f56f62c593fcc403/cmd/bd/test_dolt_server_cgo_test.go#L15-L39)
- [offline workflow environment](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/.github/workflows/proxied-local-smoke.yml#L71-L88)

This is observable, not theoretical:

- the exact local smoke invocation started and stopped
  `dolthub/dolt-sql-server:2.2.0` before the managed-local test;
- the hosted CI log avoided startup only because that image was not cached:
  `WARN: Docker image ... not cached locally ..., skipping Dolt tests`.

Docker is reached through its Unix socket, so the daemon is outside the
test process's network namespace. A cached image makes this allegedly
testcontainer-free lane environment-dependent and allows unrelated Docker
setup to delay or fail before the target test.

**Fix:** exempt `BEADS_TEST_PROXIED_LOCAL=1` in
`startTestDoltServer`, and set `BEADS_TEST_SKIP=dolt` in the workflow as a
defense-in-depth assertion of intent.

## Important test-strengthening fixes

### P2 — Dynamic listener checks do not bind the pidfile ports to the pidfile processes

`assertLoopbackOnlyListeners` requires at least one listener and rejects
non-loopback addresses, but it never receives the expected `proxyPF.Port` or
`backendPF.Port`. A PID with an unrelated loopback listener passes even when the
advertised serving port belongs to another process.

See [the calls](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L94-L102)
and [the assertion](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L175-L190).

**Fix:** require the enumerated socket set to contain the expected
`127.0.0.1:<pidfile port>` for each process, in addition to rejecting every
non-loopback listener.

### P2 — The requested idle timeout is not asserted

The test requests five seconds, but `bdManagedLocalInit` only checks that the
client-info `External` block is absent. It never asserts
`info.IdleTimeout == 5*time.Second`. The shutdown wait permits 90 seconds, so a
regression that drops the flag and falls back to the 30-second production
default still passes.

See [client-info validation](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_helpers_test.go#L52-L72)
and [timeout setup/wait](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/blob/9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f/cmd/bd/proxied_local_lifecycle_linux_test.go#L44-L56).

**Fix:** assert the persisted timeout immediately after init. If the held
connection is intended to prove active-connection gating end to end, hold and
query it across more than one full configured idle interval before release.

## Non-blocking hardening

- Use context-bounded subprocess helpers. The outer 60-second poll deadline
  does not bound `bdProxiedRun`, which uses an unbounded `exec.Command.Run`;
  a wedged child reaches the 15-minute global timeout and can strand detached
  proxy/backend processes.
- Remove the `proxyPF2.Pid != proxyPF.Pid` assertion or compare process birth
  identities. Linux may legitimately reuse a numeric PID; number inequality is
  not an identity contract.
- Add table tests for the roughly 100-line procfs parser, especially IPv6,
  mapped addresses, malformed rows, and absent `/proc/net/tcp6`.
- Upload `server.log`, generated config, pidfiles, and socket snapshots on
  workflow failure. The current `t.TempDir` cleanup discards the most useful
  diagnostics for an intermittent lifecycle failure.
- Consider concurrency cancellation for superseded PR runs.

## Existing product risks are not blockers for this test-only PR

The review also traced pre-existing PID reuse, stale pidfile, backend
port-readiness, and shutdown races in the managed proxy implementation. PR
#5004 does not introduce them, and its body explicitly leaves identity
hardening to [#4513](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/issues/4513) and
[#4637](https://github.com/gastownhall/beads/issues/4637). They should remain
separate product work rather than expanding this test-only PR into a lifecycle
redesign.

The smoke can, however, cheaply improve future coverage by checking exact
pidfile-port ownership and the restarted backend identity. Those assertions
strengthen this lane without pretending to solve the underlying identity
protocol.

## What held up under adversarial review

- `External == nil`, proxied subprocess environment selection, and direct SQL
  through the advertised proxy jointly rule out an embedded/external false
  positive.
- Closing both the checked-out `sql.Conn` and its entire `sql.DB` pool is the
  correct way to release every proxy TCP session before idle shutdown.
- Cleanup is registered before init launches the detached topology.
- The graceful path checks both pidfile removal and death of the recorded proxy
  and backend processes.
- Static generated-config validation, live socket enumeration, CLI CRUD,
  direct SQL, idle shutdown, restart, and persisted reread provide complementary
  signal.
- The `/proc/net/tcp{,6}` inode join and address decoding are sound for the
  current Linux/amd64 lane.
- `git diff --check` is clean; formatting and lint CI passed.

## Validation and PR state observed

- Base `main` health: green under blocking preflight.
- Preflight: branch maintainable and mergeable, but review required and checks
  pending at review start.
- Hosted managed-local lane: passed in 21.8 seconds on Dolt 2.2.2.
- Local exact smoke: three consecutive passes on Dolt 2.2.0, 66.1 seconds
  total; package setup unexpectedly started the shared Docker Dolt container.
- CGO-off falsification: green zero-test reproduction confirmed.
- PR discussion/review threads: none at review time.
- Working tree at exact head `9bcfcbff025007dfe6de06ed3e12f2214c59e58f`
  remained clean.

## Recommended maintainer action

Apply the three blocker fixes directly on `test/proxied-local-smoke`, strengthen
the exact-port and timeout assertions in the same pass, rerun the hosted lane,
and obtain a substantive review from someone other than the author. The PR is
then a strong merge candidate.

Do not open a replacement PR: the contributor branch is writable, the design
and most of the implementation are worth preserving, and every blocker is
localized.

---

payload.message:
Verdict: **Fix-merge — do not merge PR #5004 as-is.**

Three blockers:

- CGO-off builds silently pass with zero tests.
- Workflow path filters omit direct managed-local dependencies and `merge_group`.
- The “testcontainer-free” lane still enters shared Docker setup when the image is cached.

Also strengthen exact pidfile-port ownership and idle-timeout persistence assertions.

Validation:

- Exact smoke passed 3/3 locally.
- Hosted lane passed in 21.8 seconds.
- CGO-off false-green reproduced.
- Base `main` is green; 43 PR checks remained pending, none failed.

Full report: [pr-5004-adversarial-review-2026-07-23.md](/var/home/matt/dev/mybd/.worktrees/mybd/pr-5004-adversarial/reports/pr-5004-adversarial-review-2026-07-23.md)

Report commit `050cb91c1` is pushed on `review/pr-5004-adversarial`. Follow-up is tracked as `mybd-gsce`. Nothing was posted or changed upstream.
